_comment: OpenObserve v0.91.1 alert rules — reshaped to the v2 alert schema (GOL-278).
_lineage: Consumed by scripts/setup-monitoring.py -> POST/PUT /api/v2/{org}/alerts (upsert by name). Each references the shared `keep-webhook` destination (openobserve/keep-destination.json), which renders the keep-event template and POSTs to Keep. Keep's workflows route by context_attributes.severity.
_validation_note: Stream + field names encode intent from the prior abstract spec and are PENDING LIVE VALIDATION against first real traffic from each telemetry source (synthetic-runner, otel-collector hostmetrics/docker_stats, beyla RED, postgresql receiver, DO cost/app bridges, RUM). OO accepts alerts on not-yet-existing streams, so all upsert now and begin evaluating once their source ships. The monitor-based availability alerts (frontend/odoo/ghost/postgres/ssl) depend on an uptime metric the synthetic-runner/blackbox source must emit — that source is a follow-up.

alerts.name: frontend-down-warning
alerts.description: [{tenant}] {route} degraded — status={status_code} duration={duration_ms}ms. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#frontend-degraded  [intent: status != 2xx for >60s OR response time >5s for >2 checks]

alerts.name: frontend-down-critical
alerts.description: [{tenant}] FRONTEND DOWN — root endpoint unreachable for {duration_s}s. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#frontend-down  [intent: monitor unreachable (timeout or connection refused) for >30s]

alerts.name: odoo-down-critical
alerts.description: ODOO BACKEND DOWN — /web/login unreachable for {duration_s}s. All storefront cart + checkout flows will fail. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#odoo-down  [intent: monitor unreachable for >30s]

alerts.name: odoo-degraded-warning
alerts.description: Odoo degraded — status={status_code} duration={duration_ms}ms. Storefronts may show empty product lists. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#odoo-degraded  [intent: status != 2xx,303 for >60s OR response time >10s for >3 checks]

alerts.name: ghost-down-warning
alerts.description: [{tenant}] Ghost admin API unreachable — /blog page will fail. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#ghost-down  [intent: monitor unreachable for >60s]  [note: Ghost is content-only; degraded Ghost doesn't break commerce, hence warning not critical.]

alerts.name: postgres-down-critical
alerts.description: POSTGRES DOWN — TCP probe to postgres:5432 failed for {duration_s}s. Odoo will be unable to serve any request. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#postgres-down  [intent: TCP probe failed for >30s]

alerts.name: ssl-expiring-warning
alerts.description: [{host}] SSL cert expires in {days_remaining} days. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#ssl-expiring  [intent: days_until_expiry < 14]  [deferred: Local has no real certs. SSL monitors will fail-to-evaluate locally; alert is meaningful only once M2 droplet exists with real certs.]

alerts.name: synthetic-health-critical
alerts.description: SYNTHETIC health failing — grove_headless /health is down (Odoo API unreachable or module unloaded). All storefront commerce fails. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#odoo-down  [intent: journey='health' success==0 for >2 consecutive runs]  [note: Tier-1 synthetic (Hurl). Queries the synthetic_journey_success metric stream emitted by the synthetic-runner, not a built-in monitor.]

alerts.name: synthetic-cart-critical
alerts.description: [{tenant}] SYNTHETIC cart-flow failing — add-to-cart → cart-get broke. The money path is degraded. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#cart-broken  [intent: journey='cart-flow' success==0 for >2 consecutive runs (any tenant)]

alerts.name: synthetic-catalog-warning
alerts.description: [{tenant}] SYNTHETIC catalog failing — product list/detail returned no usable data. Storefront may show empty shop. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#catalog-empty  [intent: journey='catalog' success==0 for >2 consecutive runs (any tenant)]

alerts.name: synthetic-latency-warning
alerts.description: [{tenant}] SYNTHETIC {journey} slow — p95 {duration_ms}ms over 5m (threshold 3000ms). Backend degrading. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#synthetic-slow  [intent: p95(duration_ms) > 3000 over 5m (any journey/tenant)]

alerts.name: synthetic-checkout-critical
alerts.description: [{tenant}] SYNTHETIC checkout-canary failing — the /orders bearer endpoint or the access_token gate is broken. Orders cannot be created. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#checkout-broken  [intent: journey='checkout-canary' success==0 for >2 consecutive runs (any tenant)]  [note: Only fires when SYNTHETIC_CANARY_ENABLED=true (the canary journey runs). Tests the money-path write + the PII access_token gate end-to-end.]

alerts.name: cost-budget-warning
alerts.description: COST — month-to-date spend ${mtd} is >80% of the ${budget}/mo budget. Review the rightsizing dashboard. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#cost-budget  [intent: cost_account_month_to_date > 0.8 * COST_MONTHLY_BUDGET]  [note: CostOps (DO billing bridge). Threshold uses COST_MONTHLY_BUDGET from .env.monitoring; disabled when budget=0. Only fires when COST_BRIDGE_ENABLED=true.]

alerts.name: cost-budget-critical
alerts.description: COST — month-to-date spend ${mtd} has EXCEEDED the ${budget}/mo budget. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#cost-budget  [intent: cost_account_month_to_date > COST_MONTHLY_BUDGET]

alerts.name: cost-anomaly-warning
alerts.description: COST — daily spend spiked >2x baseline (a resource was scaled/added?). Check cost_resource_monthly_estimate for the mover. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#cost-anomaly  [intent: day-over-day cost_account_month_to_date_usage delta > 2x trailing-7d avg daily delta]

alerts.name: synthetic-ghost-content-warning
alerts.description: [{tenant}] SYNTHETIC ghost-content failing — Ghost Content API returned no posts (key rotated/invalid or blog empty). The tenant /blog page will fail. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#ghost-content-empty  [intent: journey='ghost-content' success==0 for >2 consecutive runs (any tenant)]  [note: Only fires when SYNTHETIC_GHOST_ENABLED=true. Content-level depth beyond the ghost-*-admin availability monitors; warning (Ghost is content-only, doesn't break commerce).]

alerts.name: droplet-cpu-warning
alerts.description: USE — droplet CPU >70% for 10m. Identify the hot container via docker_stats. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#droplet-cpu  [intent: avg(system_cpu_utilization) > 0.70 over 10m]  [note: USE metric from the OTel Collector (hostmetrics). Utilization is a 0-1 fraction. Stream/field name pending live validation.]  [scope: host_role='odoo-droplet' (GOL-76) so it does not blend with the agenticos box sharing this stream]

alerts.name: droplet-cpu-critical
alerts.description: USE — droplet CPU >90% for 10m. Imminent thrash risk. Restart non-critical containers or scale. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#droplet-cpu  [intent: avg(system_cpu_utilization) > 0.90 over 10m]  [scope: host_role='odoo-droplet' (GOL-76) so it does not blend with the agenticos box sharing this stream]

alerts.name: container-ram-warning
alerts.description: USE — [{container_name}] memory >80% of limit for 5m. Odoo workers most likely. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#container-ram  [intent: container_memory_percent > 80 for 5m (any container)]  [scope: host_role='odoo-droplet' (GOL-76) so it does not blend with the agenticos box sharing this stream]

alerts.name: container-ram-critical
alerts.description: USE — [{container_name}] memory >95% of limit — OOM kill imminent. Restart it. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#container-ram  [intent: container_memory_percent > 95 for 5m (any container)]  [scope: host_role='odoo-droplet' (GOL-76) so it does not blend with the agenticos box sharing this stream]

alerts.name: disk-data-warning
alerts.description: USE — data filesystem >75% full. MinIO objects or Postgres WAL growing. Clean up or add volume. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#disk-data  [intent: system_filesystem_utilization{mountpoint~=/data|/} > 0.75]  [scope: host_role='odoo-droplet' (GOL-76) so it does not blend with the agenticos box sharing this stream]

alerts.name: disk-data-critical
alerts.description: USE — data filesystem >90% full — near-full kills MinIO writes + Postgres WAL. Immediate action. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#disk-data  [intent: system_filesystem_utilization{mountpoint~=/data|/} > 0.90]  [note: Tighter than typical (75/90) because MinIO + Postgres WAL fail silently-then-catastrophically when the volume fills. postgres-connections + the RED latency/5xx alerts need the postgresql receiver + Beyla (follow-ups).]  [scope: host_role='odoo-droplet' (GOL-76) so it does not blend with the agenticos box sharing this stream]

alerts._comment: ── agenticos-* USE alerts (GOL-76) ── v2 schema (per GOL-278 contract), scoped host_role='agenticos', consuming the GOL-54 OTel Collector streams (AgenticOS repo otelcol-agenticos.yaml → host.role=agenticos). Host RAM (system_memory_utilization) is the primary signal (paperclip-server OOM box). host_role column + thresholds PENDING LIVE VALIDATION once the agenticos collector ships to grove-obs.

alerts.name: agenticos-cpu-warning
alerts.description: USE — agenticos CPU >70% for 10m. Identify the hot container via docker_stats (paperclip-server runs agent subprocesses). Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#agenticos-cpu  [intent: avg(system_cpu_utilization{host_role='agenticos'}) > 0.70 over 10m]  [note: host_role='agenticos' scopes this to the AgenticOS box; consumes the GOL-54 otelcol-agenticos.yaml streams. host_role column name (flat vs nested) PENDING LIVE VALIDATION on first agenticos traffic.]

alerts.name: agenticos-cpu-critical
alerts.description: USE — agenticos CPU >90% for 10m. Imminent thrash risk. Restart non-critical containers or resize (GOL-51). Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#agenticos-cpu  [intent: avg(system_cpu_utilization{host_role='agenticos'}) > 0.90 over 10m]  [note: host_role='agenticos' scopes this to the AgenticOS box; consumes the GOL-54 otelcol-agenticos.yaml streams. host_role column name (flat vs nested) PENDING LIVE VALIDATION on first agenticos traffic.]

alerts.name: agenticos-memory-warning
alerts.description: USE — agenticos host RAM >70% for 10m. The OOM history (103% crash) lives here — watch swap (paging.utilization) and the top container. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#agenticos-memory  [intent: avg(system_memory_utilization{host_role='agenticos'}) > 0.70 over 10m]  [note: host_role='agenticos' scopes this to the AgenticOS box; consumes the GOL-54 otelcol-agenticos.yaml streams. host_role column name (flat vs nested) PENDING LIVE VALIDATION on first agenticos traffic.]

alerts.name: agenticos-memory-critical
alerts.description: USE — agenticos host RAM >90% for 10m — OOM kill imminent (this box crashed the UI at 103%). Swap is the safety net; restart the hot container or resize (GOL-51). Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#agenticos-memory  [intent: avg(system_memory_utilization{host_role='agenticos'}) > 0.90 over 10m]  [note2: host-level RAM = the AgenticOS box's primary failure mode; DO-native monitor-alerts.tf (P0) is the redundant fast layer.]  [note: host_role='agenticos' scopes this to the AgenticOS box; consumes the GOL-54 otelcol-agenticos.yaml streams. host_role column name (flat vs nested) PENDING LIVE VALIDATION on first agenticos traffic.]

alerts.name: agenticos-container-ram-warning
alerts.description: USE — agenticos [{container_name}] memory >80% of limit for 5m. paperclip-server (agent subprocesses) most likely. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#agenticos-container-ram  [intent: container_memory_percent{host_role='agenticos'} > 80 for 5m (any container)]  [note: host_role='agenticos' scopes this to the AgenticOS box; consumes the GOL-54 otelcol-agenticos.yaml streams. host_role column name (flat vs nested) PENDING LIVE VALIDATION on first agenticos traffic.]

alerts.name: agenticos-container-ram-critical
alerts.description: USE — agenticos [{container_name}] memory >95% of limit — OOM kill imminent. Restart it. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#agenticos-container-ram  [intent: container_memory_percent{host_role='agenticos'} > 95 for 5m (any container)]  [note: host_role='agenticos' scopes this to the AgenticOS box; consumes the GOL-54 otelcol-agenticos.yaml streams. host_role column name (flat vs nested) PENDING LIVE VALIDATION on first agenticos traffic.]

alerts.name: agenticos-disk-warning
alerts.description: USE — agenticos root filesystem >75% full. Docker images/logs or the swap file growing. Prune or add volume. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#agenticos-disk  [intent: system_filesystem_utilization{host_role='agenticos',mountpoint~=/} > 0.75]  [note: host_role='agenticos' scopes this to the AgenticOS box; consumes the GOL-54 otelcol-agenticos.yaml streams. host_role column name (flat vs nested) PENDING LIVE VALIDATION on first agenticos traffic.]

alerts.name: agenticos-disk-critical
alerts.description: USE — agenticos root filesystem >90% full — a full disk stalls Docker + the agent runtime. Immediate cleanup. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#agenticos-disk  [intent: system_filesystem_utilization{host_role='agenticos',mountpoint~=/} > 0.90]  [note: host_role='agenticos' scopes this to the AgenticOS box; consumes the GOL-54 otelcol-agenticos.yaml streams. host_role column name (flat vs nested) PENDING LIVE VALIDATION on first agenticos traffic.]

alerts.name: odoo-orders-latency-warning
alerts.description: RED — Odoo /orders p95 >3s over 5m. Check Postgres connections + slow query log. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#odoo-latency  [intent: p95(http_server_request_duration) where url_path='/grove/api/v1/orders' > 3s over 5m]  [note: RED metric from Beyla eBPF (Odoo). Metric stream/field names (http_server_request_duration, url_path) pending live validation on first Beyla run.]

alerts.name: odoo-orders-latency-critical
alerts.description: RED — Odoo /orders p95 >8s — orders timing out. Check Postgres locks; restart Odoo if no DB cause. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#odoo-latency  [intent: p95(http_server_request_duration) where url_path='/grove/api/v1/orders' > 8s over 5m]

alerts.name: odoo-products-latency-warning
alerts.description: RED — Odoo /products p95 >1s. Cache miss or Postgres scan; check KeyDB hit rate. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#odoo-latency  [intent: p95(http_server_request_duration) where url_path='/grove/api/v1/products' > 1s over 5m]

alerts.name: odoo-error-rate-warning
alerts.description: RED — Odoo 5xx rate >1% over 5m. Check Odoo worker logs for Python exceptions. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#odoo-error-rate  [intent: count(http.response.status_code>=500) / count(*) on Odoo > 1% over 5m]  [note: Beyla RED. nextjs-* latency/5xx alerts need Next.js instrumentation.ts (grove-sites) — separate PR.]

alerts.name: postgres-connections-warning
alerts.description: USE — Postgres connections >70% of max. Check for idle-in-transaction queries; likely an Odoo worker leak. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#postgres-connections  [intent: postgresql_backends / postgresql_connection_max > 0.70]  [note: USE metric from the OTel Collector `postgresql` receiver — OFF until a read-only pg_monitor user + POSTGRES_* env are set and the receiver is added to the metrics pipeline (see otel/README.md). Stream/field names pending live validation.]

alerts.name: postgres-connections-critical
alerts.description: USE — Postgres connections >90% of max — new connections will be refused. Restart Odoo to clear leaked connections. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#postgres-connections  [intent: postgresql_backends / postgresql_connection_max > 0.90]

alerts.name: app-cpu-warning
alerts.description: USE — App Platform [{app}/{component}] CPU >70% for 10m. Consider a larger instance size or +1 instance. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#app-cpu  [intent: avg(do_app_cpu_percentage) > 70 over 10m (any app component)]  [note: USE metric from the DO-metrics bridge (App Platform). Percentage is 0-100 (NOT the 0-1 hostmetrics fraction). Stream/field name pending live validation.]

alerts.name: app-cpu-critical
alerts.description: USE — App Platform [{app}/{component}] CPU >90% for 10m — requests will queue/latency spikes. Scale now. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#app-cpu  [intent: avg(do_app_cpu_percentage) > 90 over 10m (any app component)]

alerts.name: app-memory-warning
alerts.description: USE — App Platform [{app}/{component}] memory >80% for 5m. Watch for OOM restarts (see app-restarts). Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#app-memory  [intent: avg(do_app_memory_percentage) > 80 over 5m (any app component)]

alerts.name: app-memory-critical
alerts.description: USE — App Platform [{app}/{component}] memory >95% for 5m — OOM kill imminent. Bump instance size. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#app-memory  [intent: avg(do_app_memory_percentage) > 95 over 5m (any app component)]

alerts.name: app-restarts-warning
alerts.description: USE — App Platform [{app}/{component}] restarted in the last 10m — possible crash loop or OOM. Check the app's deploy/runtime logs. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#app-restarts  [intent: max(do_app_restart_count) > 0 over 10m (any app component)]

alerts.name: rum-lcp-warning
alerts.description: RUM — real-user LCP p75 >2.5s over 30m. Largest Contentful Paint is slow; check hero/image payloads + Odoo BFF latency. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#rum-web-vitals  [intent: p75(web_vitals.value where metric='LCP') > 2500 over 30m (per tenant)]  [note: Real User Monitoring from @grove/analytics (grove-sites). web_vitals events are emitted to OpenObserve RUM via addAction with {metric, value, rating}. CLS value is scaled ×1000. Stream/field names pending live validation on first real RUM traffic.]

alerts.name: rum-inp-warning
alerts.description: RUM — real-user INP p75 >200ms over 30m. Interaction latency is poor; check hydration cost + heavy client-side handlers. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#rum-web-vitals  [intent: p75(web_vitals.value where metric='INP') > 200 over 30m (per tenant)]

alerts.name: rum-cls-warning
alerts.description: RUM — real-user CLS p75 >0.1 over 30m. Layout shift is high; check images/embeds/fonts without reserved space. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#rum-web-vitals  [intent: p75(web_vitals.value where metric='CLS') > 100 over 30m (per tenant) — value is scaled ×1000, so 100 == CLS 0.1]

alerts.name: rum-purchase-drop-warning
alerts.description: RUM — real users reaching checkout but 0 completed purchases in 1h (≥5 begin_checkout, 0 purchase). The money path may be broken for real traffic — cross-check synthetic-checkout-critical. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#checkout-broken  [intent: count(begin_checkout) >= 5 AND count(purchase) == 0 over 1h (any tenant)]  [note: Funnel drop from real-user events (begin_checkout → purchase, order_id_hash only — no PII). The real-traffic complement to the synthetic checkout-canary. The ≥5 floor avoids low-traffic false positives; tune once baseline volume is known.]
